objective: Catch agent shortcuts that would invert OFARM truth, promotion, twin, pack, import, and output boundaries.

cases.title: Application cannot write materialization as truth
cases.failureMode: Generated app or SDK obtains a materialized current-state object and persists it as canonical assertion/history truth.
given: A public read model envelope with qualification and traceRef exists.
The application actor has read authority but no promotion authority.
when: The app attempts to call or emulate an internal materialization-store write.
then: The attempt is refused or impossible through generated SDK/public API.
RuntimeProblem reason code is FORBIDDEN_INTERNAL_OPERATION or MATERIALIZATION_TRUTH_STORE_BLOCKED.
No canonical assertion/history record is created.

assertions.statement: No public SDK method writes materialization stores.
A trace or problem record identifies the forbidden internal write attempt.

mustFailIf: current_state table can be updated by app code

cases.title: Projection cannot be promoted as canonical evidence
cases.failureMode: A derived projection, dashboard row, or query result is attached as if it were source evidence for accepted truth.
given: A QueryExecutionResult includes derived data and traceRefs.
when: The app submits the query result as evidence supporting an accepted execution.
then: Promotion gates refuse projection-as-evidence unless source evidence references are also present and sufficient.
The response preserves the projection as derived context only.

assertions.statement: Projection inputs are not accepted as source evidence by themselves.
Source evidence gaps are returned as RuntimeProblem entries.

cases.title: Advisory result cannot satisfy Compliance Twin approval
cases.failureMode: An advisory recommendation or assistant answer is used to create a compliance-grade accepted decision.
given: Advisory output exists with advisory-only qualification.
when: The app attempts to use the advisory output as proof of compliance authorization.
then: The platform blocks compliance promotion and requires compliance-grade authority/evidence.
The advisory output remains visible only as advisory context.

assertions.statement: Advisory Twin output is not a Compliance Twin authorization.

cases.title: Pack/profile cannot mutate core OFARM meaning
cases.failureMode: A pack or local profile silently changes canonical operation-state meaning or promotion law.
given: A pack activation plan includes a semantic override.
when: The plan attempts to redefine accepted execution, evidence sufficiency, or materialization truth semantics.
then: Activation plan is blocked or marked governance-required.
No core meaning is changed by pack activation.

assertions.statement: Pack merge cannot override core semantics.
Pack activation trace identifies the conflict.

cases.title: FMIS shadow import remains candidate-only
cases.failureMode: An FMIS, machinery, or sensor import is accepted as truth merely because it was ingested.
given: ImportCandidate includes source fidelity, unresolved mappings, and candidate status.
when: The app requests accepted execution or compliance output based only on the candidate.
then: The request is blocked or review-required until governed promotion succeeds.
Candidate receipt, source fidelity, and loss map remain attached.

assertions.statement: Candidate-only state is preserved through import result and UI.
No accepted consequence is created directly from import receipt.

cases.title: AI output cannot become a governance decision
cases.failureMode: An LLM recommendation, tool hint, or generated summary is promoted directly to authority, approval, or accepted fact.
given: AI-generated content is available in app workflow.
when: The app sends it as an accepted compliance fact or authority decision without governed review.
then: The platform refuses direct promotion and requires explicit human/policy authority gates.
The AI output can remain as draft/advisory context only.

assertions.statement: AI output is never a governance decision by default.
Authority trace points to governed actor/policy, not prompt output.

cases.title: Stale materialization blocks high-consequence output
cases.failureMode: A high-consequence DocumentAssembly or compliance decision is created from stale current state.
given: MaterializationResult has stale or invalidated qualification.
then: Assembly preview blocks or requires annex/reconstruction from source history.
The stale state is displayed as stale, not current truth.

assertions.statement: High-consequence output cannot rely on stale materialization by default.
Result qualification is carried into output preview.

cases.title: Compiled output is not a truth store
cases.failureMode: A PassportView or DocumentAssembly is re-imported as canonical source truth without source-history basis.
given: A compiled output exists with assembly trace.
when: The app imports or cites the compiled output as a new accepted source fact.
then: Platform rejects compiled-output-as-truth unless underlying source references are used and governed.

assertions.statement: Compiled outputs are presentation/assembly artifacts, not canonical truth stores.

cases.title: Workflow state collapse is detected
cases.failureMode: Generated UI labels recommendation, prescription, work order, operation claim, and accepted execution as one 'done' state.
when: App renders multiple canonical states under one final label.
then: Conformance fails unless each state remains separately visible and traceable.

assertions.statement: UI labels preserve canonical state distinctions.

cases.title: Permission-limited data is not fabricated or over-disclosed
cases.failureMode: App displays no data, fabricated data, or redacted data details when the query result is permission-limited.
when: App displays treatment history or evidence details.
then: UI states that data is restricted and does not fabricate or reveal hidden content.

assertions.statement: Permission-limited state survives API -> SDK -> UI.

acceptanceCriteria: All BLOCKER cases pass before any runtime is called AI-agent-ready.
No case may be waived by passing protocol/API validation alone.
notes: Protocol conformance cannot waive semantic-law blockers.
